vessels.name: Littorio (later Italia)
vessels.fate: Survived war; scuttled at La Spezia 1948
vessels.notes: Italy's most powerful battleship. Damaged twice by British torpedoes at Taranto (1940) and by a torpedo-bomber attack (1941). Renamed Italia in 1943 after Mussolini's fall; surrendered to the Allies.

vessels.name: Vittorio Veneto
vessels.notes: Participated in the Battle of Cape Matapan (1941) where it was torpedoed and barely escaped. Surrendered at Malta in September 1943.

vessels.name: Zara
vessels.notes: Sunk by British destroyers at the Battle of Cape Matapan after being disabled. The battle was a decisive Italian defeat — three heavy cruisers and two destroyers lost in a night action.

vessels.name: Adua (submarine)
vessels.notes: Representative of Italy's large submarine fleet — over 100 boats at war's start. Italian submarines operated extensively in the Mediterranean and Atlantic; they sank over 500,000 GRT of Allied shipping.
